subtitle: A mile-and-a-half arc of white marble pebbles below two mountains, voted Greece's best beach a dozen times — and one of the country's most lethal places to swim.
spike_statement: The white marble that makes Myrtos glow turquoise also makes its water deepen to drowning depth a few steps from shore.

levers.photo_tone: marble-white pebble against banded Ionian blue, cliffs in hard noon shadow
